name: Jordan — Design Hiring Manager
role: Head of Design or Senior Product Designer leading hiring at a mid-size product company (50–500 employees, Series B or later)
goals: Fill a mid-level UX/Product Designer role without a long sourcing cycle
Find someone who can ship — not just design in Figma but hand off and iterate with engineers
Confirm the candidate has real research ownership, not just participation
Reduce risk: hire someone who has worked in a similar context (enterprise SaaS or startup) and can ramp quickly
Confirm the candidate understands business context, not just user pain
frustrations: Portfolios that show only polished final screens — no process, no decisions, no tradeoffs
Case studies where the designer says 'we' for everything — can't tell what they actually owned
Long case studies that take 10 minutes to read and bury the outcome at the bottom
Designers who can't explain why they made a design decision, only what they made
Portfolios that look like Figma template showcases rather than real shipped product
No quantified outcomes — 'improved usability' with no number attached
Case studies that bury outcomes at the bottom after 10 minutes of process documentation
Metrics that sound inflated or forced rather than earned
Portfolios with identical case study structure on every project regardless of context
behaviors: Spends 30–60 seconds on the first pass — skims for signal: outcomes, company names, product type
Clicks one case study — usually the most recent or the most recognizable company
Reads the intro and the outcome first, then scrolls through process if they're still interested
Looks for evidence of research ownership: screeners, moderated sessions, affinity mapping
Checks if the designer understood the problem before they opened Figma
Forwards the portfolio link to a PM or lead engineer before the panel interview
Compares 15–30 portfolios per role — attention is scarce
Looks at homepage cards for signal before clicking into any case study
Reads outcomes and intro first, scrolls through process only if still interested
Compares against 15-30 other portfolios for the same role
Forwards portfolio to PM or lead engineer before panel if it passes the first screen
tools_used: Direct portfolio URL shared by recruiter
Figma (to review design files during technical screen)
Slack (to share candidate impressions with team)
quote: I can tell in 45 seconds if someone has shipped real product or just designed pretty screens. Show me what you learned, not just what you built.
